mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within 0.1%) in more than 40% of periods following a positive RSAFS growth signal.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often follow unemployment spikes
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is rising while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, it indicates a hawkish monetary policy stance aimed at curbing inflation, which typically tightens financial conditions and negatively impacts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where FEDFUNDS is rising and CPIAUCSL is above 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, companies are expanding output, signaling robust economic activity and corporate earnings, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short, one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is negative during periods where INDPRO growth exceeds 3% annualized, or if the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which acts as a leading indicator for broader economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises by > 10% from previous 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of instances following a significant rise in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer spending capacity, which underpins corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected hike in the Federal Funds Rate signals a more aggressive monetary tightening than anticipated, which can shock markets by increasing borrowing costs and reducing future earnings expectations, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:market reaction to unexpected rate hikes
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a single-month FEDFUNDS increase of over 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and robust retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength, signaling a healthy demand environment that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS YoY growth > 4%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong growth.
